content.cn_text: 下午好。这是野生动物系列讲座的第三讲。对于
content.en_text: Good afternoon. This is the third session of the series of lectures on wildlife. For

content.cn_text: 本次讲座，我们将看两项关于鸟类栖息地和保护
content.en_text: this lecture, we're going to look at two studies on the habitats of birds and protection

content.cn_text: 措施的澳大利亚研究，包括基本方法和相关研究领域。
content.en_text: measures taken in Australia, including the basic methodology and related areas of research.

content.cn_text: 现在，我来介绍一下第一个鸟类研究项目的背景信息。
content.en_text: Now, let me give you some background information about the first research project on birds.

content.cn_text: 这项研究由RB Cunningham及其来自澳大利亚国立
content.en_text: The study was carried out by RB Cunningham, and his team from the Australian National

content.cn_text: 大学的团队于1995年开展。主要目标是探测一些鸟类的繁殖习性和休息地点，
content.en_text: University in 1995. The primary goal was to detect the breeding habits and resting sites

content.cn_text: 尤其是城市地区的那些受保护的物种。这些物种包括
content.en_text: of some birds, especially those protected species in urban regions. These species included

content.cn_text: 澳大利亚的游隼和椋鸟等鸟类。鸟类数据集包含
content.en_text: birds like Peregrine Falcon and Starling in Australia. The bird data set consisted

content.cn_text: 来自946个地点记录到的共153个物种。人们可能会以为，为了
content.en_text: of a total of 153 species recorded from 946 sites. One might expect that in order to

content.cn_text: 获得它们分布模式和种群密度的有效数据，研究人员必须
content.en_text: obtain valid data on their distribution patterns and population densities, researchers had

content.cn_text: 通过双筒望远镜进行计数。相反，他们设法用各种标准方法进行估算，
content.en_text: to count through binoculars. Instead, they managed to estimate with various standard

content.cn_text: 例如随机抽样等距离抽样方法。此外，他们使用了一个绘图系统来
content.en_text: distance sampling methods like random sampling. In addition, a mapping system was used to

content.cn_text: 研究鸟类的伴侣行为。它记录了目标鸟类可能的筑巢地点，以监测
content.en_text: study their companionship behavior. It recorded proposed birds' nesting sites to monitor the

content.cn_text: 目标物种的交配行为。为了获得鸟类行踪的有效数据，研究人员
content.en_text: mating behavior of targeted species. To obtain valid data of birds' whereabouts, researchers

content.cn_text: 在鸟腿上安装能传输无线电信号的身份标签。标记过程是在
content.en_text: attached identity tags to bird's legs transmitting radio signals. The tagging process was done when

content.cn_text: 鸟类出生5到7天之间完成的。初次标记后，数据从
content.en_text: the birds were between five and seven days of age. After the initial tagging, data was collected

content.cn_text: 带无线电标签的鸟类子集中收集，使研究人员能够评估鸟类在15
content.en_text: from subsets of radio-tagged birds, making it possible to assess the trends of birds over 15

content.cn_text: 个月内的趋势。那么，他们发现了什么？结果表明，许多被标记的鸟倾向于
content.en_text: months. So, what did they find? Well, as it turned out, many of the birds tagged had the tendency

content.cn_text: 在它们长大的同一栖息地筑巢。它们的筑巢地点相当多样，包括
content.en_text: to nest in the same habitat where they grew up. Their nesting sites were quite varied and included

content.cn_text: 淡水芦苇床、潮汐芦苇床、农作物区和人工地点。尽管自然
content.en_text: freshwater reed beds, tidal reed beds, agricultural crops and man-made sites. Even though naturally

content.cn_text: 形成的巢很常见，但鸟类有时也会意外地在人工设施中筑巢，比如
content.en_text: occurring nests were common, birds sometimes accidentally nested in man-made devices like

content.cn_text: 电塔、烟囱和隧道。这项研究还研究了人类对鸟类
content.en_text: power towers, chimneys and tunnels. This research also studied the impact of humans on bird

content.cn_text: 群落的影响，并发现污染极大地导致了一些物种出生率下降，
content.en_text: communities, and found out that pollution had contributed greatly to the declining birth rates

content.cn_text: 这些物种被认为已经灭绝，或面临从研究区域消失的威胁。
content.en_text: of some species, which were considered to be extinct or threatened to vanish from the study area.

content.cn_text: 这些物种依赖天然森林，因为它们需要繁殖洞穴。如果当地天然森林继续
content.en_text: These species are dependent on natural forests because of their breeding holes. They will

content.cn_text: 受到严重污染，它们将会灭绝。因此，当地动物保护组织成员采取了许多
content.en_text: become extinct when local natural forests continue to be polluted heavily. A number of

content.cn_text: 措施来提高它们令人担忧的
content.en_text: measures were thus taken by members from local animal conservation organizations to boost their

content.cn_text: 存活率。首先，人们把繁殖箱放在树上，用来代替
content.en_text: alarming survival rates. Firstly, breeding boxes were placed in trees taking on the role of breeding

content.cn_text: 被污染的繁殖洞穴。保护指南也被引入，严格禁止捕杀、
content.en_text: holes that were contaminated. Protection guidelines were also introduced to strictly prohibiting killing,

content.cn_text: 干扰和破坏栖息地。进一步的措施是确保鸟类免受
content.en_text: disturbance and habitat destruction. A further measure was taken to ensure the safety of birds

content.cn_text: 风力发电场的危害。事实上，鸟类有时会被风力涡轮叶片伤害。大量
content.en_text: from wind farms. In fact, birds are sometimes shredded by wind turbine blades. A great deal of

content.cn_text: 工作都集中在如何防止它们与涡轮机相撞。一个典型例子是保护
content.en_text: work was done on how to prevent them from colliding with turbines. A case in point was protecting

content.cn_text: 鸟类迁徙空间，确保迁徙路线保持畅通。猎鹰通常
content.en_text: space for bird migration by ensuring that migratory routes were kept free. Falcons generally

content.cn_text: 能较好地应对风力涡轮机，但在能见度差的天气条件下则不行。因此，借助
content.en_text: cope well with wind turbines, but not in weather conditions where visibility is bad. So, with knowledge

content.cn_text: 对猎鹰飞行速度的了解，澳大利亚北部的鸟类观测站可以通知更南边的风电场，
content.en_text: of how fast falcons fly, bird stations in northern Australia can notify wind farms further south of

content.cn_text: 告知猎鹰正在接近，这样当它们经过时，涡轮机就可以关闭。随后，另一项研究
content.en_text: the falcons approach so that turbines can be shut down as they pass by. Then another piece of research

content.cn_text: 几年后由Conway于2006年完成。他的团队检测了鸟类夜间飞行时叫声的频率。
content.en_text: was done a couple of years later by Conway in 2006. His team detected the calling frequency of

content.cn_text: 海鸟。声学信号是鸟类重要的交流工具。这些
content.en_text: sea birds flying at night. Acoustic signals are important communication tools for birds. These

content.cn_text: 信号可以显示社会凝聚、预定位和繁殖行为。求偶歌声在
content.en_text: signals can indicate social cohesion, pre-location and breeding behaviour. Courtship songs are common

content.cn_text: 各种物种的交配季节很常见。雄鸟会发出呼叫歌声来吸引远处的
content.en_text: among various species during mating seasons. Males produce calling songs that attract distant

content.cn_text: 雌鸟，然后求偶歌声会促使附近的雌鸟回应。
content.en_text: females, and then courtship songs will induce nearby females to respond.

content.cn_text: 观察人员还对检测到的所有单只鸟的声音进行了录音。他们使用平板电脑
content.en_text: Observers also took recordings of the sounds of all the individual birds detected. A tablet PC was used

content.cn_text: 处理调查中的详细信息，比如声音音高、频谱、长度和时间。为了尽量减少
content.en_text: to process detailed information like sound pitch, spectrum, length and timing of surveys. To minimise

content.cn_text: 对鸟类群落的干扰，所有声音都通过麦克风从一定距离外录制，
content.en_text: the disturbance on the bird community, all the sounds were recorded from a distance using microphones

content.cn_text: 用来检测声音。他们发现，独居鸟类发出叫声事件的概率要高得多，
content.en_text: to detect noise. They found that the probability of call events was much higher for solitary birds

content.cn_text: 尤其是在一天较早和较晚的时候进行交流。
content.en_text: communicating early and late in the day.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: It aimed to study birds which were 31. __________ in cities.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问研究对象是在城市中的什么鸟类，原文说 protected species in urban regions，因此答案是 protected。

items.title: Researchers were able to 32. __________ rather than count the number of birds by some methods, such as random sampling.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问研究人员能够做什么，而不是数鸟的数量，原文说 instead, they managed to estimate，因此答案是 estimate。

items.title: Using a 33. __________ system to study birds’ companionship habits.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问用什么系统研究鸟类的 companionship habits，原文说 a mapping system was used，因此答案是 mapping。

items.title: Research attached tags to birds’ legs to decide the 34. __________ over time.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问给鸟腿装标签是为了判断随时间变化的什么，原文说 assess the trends of birds over 15 months，因此答案是 trends。

items.title: Human activities, e.g. 35. __________, can be a disadvantage to bird reproduction.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问哪种人类活动会不利于鸟类繁殖，原文说 pollution contributed greatly to the declining birth rates，因此答案是 pollution。

items.title: To increase birds’ rate of 36. __________ in various ways.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问当地组织采取措施提高鸟类的什么 rate，原文说 boost their survival rates，因此答案是 survival。

items.title: To reduce the dangers caused by 37. __________ farms, e.g. colliding with turbines.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问减少由什么 farms 造成的危险，原文说 wind farms，并提到 colliding with turbines，因此答案是 wind。

items.title: Researchers monitored the 38. __________ of birds’ nocturnal callings during flight.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问研究人员监测鸟类夜间飞行叫声的什么，原文说 detected the calling frequency，因此答案是 frequency。

items.title: 39. __________ were taken to listen to birds’ sounds.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问为了听鸟的声音，研究人员进行了什么，原文说 took recordings of the sounds，因此答案是 recordings。

items.title: Data could be collected through noise detection devices placed at a great 40. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问数据可通过放置在很大什么处的噪声检测设备收集，原文说 recorded from a distance using microphones，因此答案是 distance。
